goal: Draft a dense reward function for Allegro in-hand cube reorientation using real IsaacLab mdp reward terms, with shaping weights exposed as an ablatable dict.
thoughts: Maya wants shaping (distance-to-target-quat, fingertip contact bonus, action-rate penalty, angular-velocity penalty) on top of existing sparse reward, no OmniGraph. Key pitfall: near identity, 1-|q1.q2| has vanishing gradient — use orientation_command_error / quat_error_magnitude from isaaclab.utils.math. Return RewardTermCfg (2.x name) entries, weights in a dict Maya can sweep.
code:
# 1. Ground in real IsaacLab reward term patterns
lookup_knowledge(query="IsaacLab 2.x RewardTermCfg mdp orientation_command_error in-hand manipulation")

# 2. Generate with explicit env spec
generate_reward(
    task_description="Allegro in-hand cube reorientation, dense shaping",
    observations=["fingertip_pos", "cube_pose", "target_quat", "joint_pos", "joint_vel", "last_action"],
    style="isaaclab_manager_based",
    terms=[
        {"name": "orientation_tracking", "fn": "orientation_command_error", "weight": "<W_ORI>"},
        {"name": "fingertip_contact",   "fn": "contact_force_threshold",  "weight": "<W_CONTACT>"},
        {"name": "action_rate",         "fn": "action_rate_l2",           "weight": "<W_ARATE>"},
        {"name": "cube_ang_vel",        "fn": "ang_vel_l2",               "weight": "<W_AVEL>"},
        {"name": "cube_drop",           "fn": "root_height_below_minimum", "weight": "<W_DROP>"}
    ]
)

# 3. Critique the generated reward before wiring it in
review_reward(reward_cfg_path="<GENERATED_REWARD_CFG>")

# 4. Verify observation term shapes match before training dry-run
run_usd_script(script="print([(t.name, t.cfg.func.__name__) for t in env.observation_manager.active_terms['policy']])")
failure_modes: Hand-rolled quaternion diff (1 - |q1.q2|) has zero gradient near identity — use quat_error_magnitude
Using RewTerm (old 1.x name) instead of RewardTermCfg (2.x) — breaks import
OmniGraph-based rewards pollute the env and Maya explicitly rejected them
Missing terminal penalty on cube drop leaves sparse signal hole at failure
